source_pr.title: planner/core: support named window
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? We need to support the named window in the planner. Ref https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/window-functions-named-windows.html ### What is changed and how it works? Before processing the window functions, we first resolve named window definitions and store it in the plan builder. After that, when processing the window functions, if it uses a named window, we will substitue the definitions for it. ### Check List Tests - Unit test Code changes - Has exported function/method change Side effects - None Related changes - None --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/pingcap/tidb/8937)

symptoms: sql: "select sum(a) over(w order by b) from t window w as (order by a)", (planner/core/logical_plan_test.go)
failure_signature: sql: "select sum(a) over(w order by b) from t window w as (order by a)", (planner/core/logical_plan_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: sql: "select sum(a) over(w order by b) from t window w as (order by a)", (planner/core/logical_plan_test.go)
